29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Rome Resources plc

Rome Resources

Total Voting Rights
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, announces that the issued share capital of the Company as at the date of this announcement comprises 7,676,625,932 ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each with one voting right per share ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company is 7,676,625,932.
The above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine if they are required to notify their interest in or a change to their interest in the share capital of the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the UK Financial Conduct Authority.
For further information, please contact:

latest.title: Airborne Geophysical Survey
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0408E
Rome Resources PLC
13 May 2026
13
May 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Airborne Geophysical Survey
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, is pleased to announce that it is participating in an airborne geophysical survey (the "Survey") covering the greater Bisie tin district, a district that contains one of the world's highest grade tin plays.
The Survey forms part of a broader contiguous programme of geophysical data acquisition being conducted across adjacent areas of the wider Bisie tin district.
Acquisition of over 600km of helicopter-borne geophysical data is planned for Rome Resources, flown by Geotech Ltd, a globally renowned geophysical contractor, at 100m line spacing and comprising a full waveform time-domain electromagnetic sensor (VTEM Plus) coupled with a caesium magnetometer.
The Survey will cover the entirety of Rome Resources' two licences and is expected to help calibrate geophysical anomalies on Rome Resources' acreage with those of nearby producing mines. It is also expected to identify and prioritise additional tin exploration targets and better define the tin granite contact to the south in Rome Resources' licence areas.
Pre-Survey flight testing commenced in the southern area on 11 May 2026 and is expected to move onto Rome Resources' licences area in late May 2026.
Paul Barrett, Chief Executive Officer of Rome Resources, commented:
"The opportunity to undertake the Survey is very timely and represents an important step in advancing our understanding of the broader Bisie mineralisation system. Alongside the assay results from our recently completed drilling campaign at Kalayi and the forthcoming updated Mineral Resource Estimate, the Survey is expected to provide critical data to guide our plans for the next phase of drilling at both Kalayi and Mont Agoma, while also helping to assess the wider potential across our two licence areas as we define the next phase of exploration at Bisie North. We look forward to sharing the results in due course."
For further information, please contact:

latest.title: Update research note published by Allenby Capital
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8524D
Rome Resources PLC
12 May 2026
REACH announcement*
12 May 2026
Rome Resources PLC
("Rome Resources" or the "Company")
Update research note published by Allenby Capital
Rome Resources Plc (AIM: RMR), the Democratic Republic of Congo
‑
focused tin and base metals explorer, highlights
the publication of a new research note by
Allenby Capital, Rome Resources' joint broker and adviser, on 6 May 2026, titled "
Bullish Backdrop
".
The note reviews Rome Resources' recent operational and corporate progress at its Bisie North project including:
·
the latest drilling programme at Kalayi where wider
and higher-grade tin intercepts at depth continue to support the Company's evolving structural model;
·
Rome Resources' proposed increase in ownership interest across the Bisie North licences,
increasing its attributable exposure to future resource growth; and
·
the upcoming Mineral Resource Estimate update, alongside
further planned exploration activity including airborne geophysics and additional drilling.
The full research note is available on Allenby Capital's website at:
https://www.allenbycapital.com/client/rome-resources-plc/

latest.title: Subscription update and additional issue of equity
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3973D
Rome Resources PLC
07 May 2026
7 May 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Update regarding additional subscription
and
further issue of equity to settle investor relation fees
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, provides the following update in respect of the Company's additional Subscription as announced on 27 April 2026 (the "
Additional Subscription
").
The Company previously announced that it expected 130,000,000 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM on or around 5 May 2026. To date, the Company has received commitments in relation to 50,000,000 new Ordinary Shares (approximately £150k) pursuant to the Additional Subscription. The Company expects the outstanding balance to be received in due course following a successful resolution of banking delays relating to the outstanding subscribers.
Additional issue of equity
Separately, the Company announces the issue of 9,600,000 new Ordinary Shares as payment in lieu of approximately £28,800 in accrued professional investor relation fees.
Admission and Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange plc for the 139,600,000 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM. It is currently anticipated that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the new Ordinary Shares will commence on AIM at 8.00 a.m. on or around 12 May 2026.
Upon Admission, Rome Resources issued ordinary share capital will comprise 7,676,625,932 Ordinary Shares. Upon Admission, this number will represent the total voting rights in the Company, and, following Admission may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Unless otherwise defined herein, the capitalised defined terms used in this announcement have the same meaning as those used in the Company's announcement on 27 April 2026.
For further information, please contact:

latest.title: Additional Subscription
latest.content:
27 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9957B
Rome Resources PLC
27 April 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310. With the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
27 April 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Additional Subscription from existing non-UK Shareholders
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, announces that, further to its announcement on 20 April 2026 regarding the conditional direct equity subscription of approximately £1.2 million (the "
Subscription
"), certain existing non-UK shareholders of the Company contacted Rome Resources requesting to participate in the financing on the same terms.
As such, the Company is pleased to announce that it has conditionally raised a further £390,000, bringing total proceeds from the Subscription to approximately £1.59 million, through the issue of 130,000,000 new ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each (the "
Additional Subscription Shares
"). The Additional Subscription Shares will be issued in certificated form and when issued and fully paid, will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares in issue and therefore will rank equally for all dividends or other distributions declared, made or paid after the issue of the Additional Subscription Shares.
Admission and Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules
​
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange Plc for the 130,000,000 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission") and it is expected that Admission will become effective on or around 5 May 2026. Upon Admission, Rome Resources issued ordinary share capital will comprise 7,667,025,932 Ordinary Shares. Upon Admission, this number will represent the total voting rights in the Company, and, following Admission may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Paul Barrett, Chief Executive Officer of Rome Resources, commented:
''We value the long-term support of these long-standing non-UK investors who have been shareholders of Rome Resources since its TSX listing and prior to the Company's migration to AIM.
Their decision to approach the Company after the initial Subscription and request participation on the same terms is a strong vote of confidence in the broader potential of our DRC projects following on from our most successful drilling campaign at Kalayi to date."
For further information, please contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: RMR Limited is a tin‑exploration company focused on the Kalayi tin project in Namibia. The firm uses seismic surveys and drilling to locate high‑grade tin deposits, which are then evaluated for commercial viability.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tin is a critical raw material for electronics, batteries, and industrial alloys. RMR’s projects sit in one of the world’s richest tin‑bearing regions, giving the company a strategic position to supply growing global demand.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the exploration phase, conducting drilling and seismic work. It has recently raised £1.2 m through a share subscription and completed a drilling program that produced promising intercepts. RMR remains a cash‑burning, pre‑revenue entity, with its shares trading at a very low valuation relative to the size of its projects.

rns_narrative: RMR has been busy on the ground, finishing a drilling campaign that yielded the widest tin intercept to date and announcing a new geophysical survey to refine the resource model. The company has also been active on the corporate side, appointing a new director, updating its subscription terms, and holding a general meeting to secure additional capital.

These events cluster into three themes: (1) **Exploration progress** – drilling and seismic data that could upgrade the resource estimate; (2) **Capital structure management** – new shares and voting rights changes aimed at strengthening the balance sheet; (3) **Market positioning** – research coverage from Allenby Capital and a proposed strategic diversification that could broaden the company’s asset base.
The narrative signals a company that is still stabilising its operations and seeking fresh capital, but the recent drilling results and a research note hint that a catalyst may be on the horizon.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with a flicker of hope
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter exists – the market remains largely indifferent, which is exactly what a contrarian looks for.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of revenue, the need for ongoing dilution, and the risk that the latest drill results could turn out lower‑grade than expected.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has stayed quiet since the last research note, with only a slight uptick in discussion after the geophysical survey announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for RMR is flat – no recent spikes in search activity.

near_term_catalysts: A formal resource estimate that confirms high‑grade tin in the Kalayi area, released in the next earnings cycle.
Positive confirmation from the new geophysical survey that extends the deposit’s depth and breadth.
Any partnership or joint‑venture announcement that brings in technical expertise or additional capital.
A significant uptick in trading volume that signals renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in volume (3–4× the 30‑day average) coupled with a price move above the latest swing high, or the release of a positive resource report. These would be the first real signs of a mean‑reversion in play.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery cycle. A clear break above recent support, coupled with a volume surge, would signal that capitulation has finished and the price is poised to bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price recovers to the halfway point of its current decline, the upside is roughly 2× the current level. The historical return for deep‑crash signals is around 40% on average, and the win‑rate for such moves is well over 40%, indicating a strong asymmetry in favour of the long side.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Further dilution from new share issues, or a disappointing resource report, could push the price down another 10–15%. The company also faces the risk that it may need to raise more capital, which would dilute existing shareholders.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of about 2× versus a capped downside that cannot fall below the current price. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore roughly 1 : 2, an attractive asymmetry for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the most recent swing high on a volume rise of at least 2× the 30‑day average. Size the position at 10–15% of the portfolio and set a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a clear volume spike and a confirmed positive resource estimate appear. A 3× volume spike on a price move above the 30‑day moving average would be a strong trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there are no imminent delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud warnings.

ai_insights.bottom_line: RMR is a deep‑crashed tin explorer that could double its price once a positive resource report is released, offering a high‑asymmetry contrarian opportunity.
